review-diff.description: Read-only review of current diff with high/medium finding ledger
review-diff.template:
Load the `global-review-ledger` skill. Review the current git diff read-only: do not edit files, stage changes, commit, or run fix commands.

Use up to ten `@reviewer` subagents in parallel only when distinct scopes are useful enough to justify fan-out. Pick scopes based on the diff and repository, preferring correctness, tests/coverage, API/contracts, security/privacy, performance/concurrency/resource lifecycle, and UX/i18n/docs/build-release when applicable.

Aggregate reviewer output into the skill's high/medium finding ledger with stable IDs, severity, file/line evidence, impact, resolution criteria, scope coverage, and verification gaps. Deduplicate repeated findings and separate low-priority or unrelated backlog notes.

If the change is small, use fewer reviewers and say which scopes were covered. Final response must be compact and read-only: findings first, then open questions, then review coverage and verification gaps.

diagnose.description: Reproduce and isolate a bug without edits
diagnose.template: Diagnose the issue from the user's prompt. Reproduce if feasible, collect minimal evidence, identify likely root cause, and recommend the smallest fix. Do not edit files.

workflow.description: Draft a project WORKFLOW.md from repository evidence
workflow.template: Load the global-project-skills skill and draft a compact project-specific WORKFLOW.md from repository evidence. Do not edit files unless explicitly asked.

harness-release-review.description: Read-only semantic release review for harness coherence
harness-release-review.template:
Load the `global-harness-release-review` skill. Review the current harness branch read-only before release: do not edit files, stage changes, commit, tag, or push.

Check whether guides and sensors remain coherent as a system: AGENTS.md, opencode.json, agents/, commands/, skills/, docs/harness-map.md, docs/evaluation.md, docs/compatibility.md, docs/release.md, scripts/, examples/, and fixtures/.

Use focused `@reviewer` subagents only when distinct semantic scopes are useful, such as guide/sensor consistency, permission safety, behaviour-contract coverage, runtime/drift coverage, release/adoption docs, and public/private boundary. Return release blockers first, then non-blocking risks, then verification evidence and gaps.
